exercises.title: Write your first function
exercises.description: Write a function named `greeting`. In the function, it should print "Hello!". Call this function.

exercises.title: Add a parameter
exercises.description: Write a function named `greetPerson`. Add a parameter to it called `name`. In the function, it should print "Greetings, " + name. Call this function with your name as input.

exercises.title: Return a value
exercises.description:
Write a function named `double`. Add a parameter called `number`. In the function, it should *return* number * 2.

 Call this function in console.log. For example:
```js
console.log(double(5));
```
This should print `10` to the console.

exercises.title: Multiple Parameters
exercises.description:
Write a function named getRectangleArea. Add two parameters, width and height. Return the area of a rectangle.

 Call this function to return an area of 100 (any two numbers that multiply to 100 is fine)

exercises.title: Max of 3
exercises.description:
Write a function named maxOfThree. Give it 3 parameters. Return the biggest of the 3 parameters.

 Call this function in a console.log. For example:
```js
console.log(maxOfThree(1, 2, 3));
```
This should print `3` to the console.

exercises.title: Create an Array
exercises.description: Create an array named 'foods'. Add 5 of your favorite foods to it (they must be STRING!)

exercises.title: Loop through Array
exercises.description:
Create an array of 5 things (numbers or strings, or anything really).
 Loop through the array and print each item in the array.

exercises.title: Index an Array
exercises.description: Create an array with at least 3 items. Print the first and third items in your array using indexes (the square bracket syntax)
